framework: King IV Report on Corporate Governance
requirement: Board Leadership, Ethics and Corporate Citizenship
findings: Board composition meets King IV requirements for independence. However, legacy governance failures linked to State Capture era require ongoing remediation. Social and Ethics Committee is functional but requires strengthening.
recommendations: Strengthen board induction programme, implement King IV governance register, enhance social and ethics reporting, conduct annual board effectiveness evaluation.

framework: Public Finance Management Act (PFMA) Act 1 of 1999
findings: Irregular expenditure of R5.8 billion identified in 2023/24. Fruitless and wasteful expenditure of R1.2 billion. Qualified audit opinion from Auditor-General. Supply chain management weaknesses persist.
recommendations: Implement consequence management for irregular expenditure, strengthen SCM controls, appoint dedicated PFMA compliance officer, develop irregular expenditure reduction plan.

findings: Board complies with Companies Act requirements for director duties. Conflict of interest register is maintained. Director declarations are filed timeously. Board charter is aligned with Companies Act.
recommendations: Enhance director training on fiduciary duties, review board charter annually, strengthen conflict of interest management procedures.

findings: Eskom holds valid generation and distribution licences from NERSA. Licence conditions are substantially met. Reporting obligations to NERSA are fulfilled. Tariff applications are submitted in accordance with regulatory timelines.
recommendations: Prepare for unbundling licence requirements, engage NERSA on transition arrangements, update licence compliance monitoring system.

framework: King IV Report on Corporate Governance
requirement: Board Leadership, Ethics and Corporate Citizenship
findings: Board has been reconstituted following State Capture era. New board demonstrates improved governance standards. However, legacy procurement irregularities require ongoing attention. Board committees are functional.
recommendations: Complete board effectiveness evaluation, strengthen ethics management programme, implement King IV governance disclosures in integrated report.

framework: Public Finance Management Act (PFMA) Act 1 of 1999
findings: Irregular expenditure of R4.2 billion identified. Qualified audit opinion received. The Zondo Commission identified Transnet as a major site of State Capture with irregular locomotive procurement contracts totalling R54 billion.
recommendations: Implement SIU recommendations, pursue civil recovery from implicated parties, strengthen procurement governance, implement consequence management.

findings: Transnet Port Terminals operates under valid port licences. Port efficiency metrics fall below regulatory benchmarks. Vessel turnaround times exceed targets. Port tariff applications are submitted to Ports Regulator.
recommendations: Improve port efficiency metrics, engage Ports Regulator on performance improvement plan, invest in port equipment and technology.

framework: King IV Report on Corporate Governance
requirement: Board Leadership, Ethics and Corporate Citizenship
findings: Post-business rescue board demonstrates improved governance. Board composition reflects appropriate skills mix. Takatso Consortium representation on board is managed in accordance with shareholder agreement.
recommendations: Develop integrated governance framework for mixed ownership structure, strengthen board induction for new Takatso nominees, implement King IV disclosures.

requirement: Domestic and International Air Service Licences
findings: SAA holds valid domestic and international air service licences. SACAA oversight requirements are met. Safety management system is certified. IATA Operational Safety Audit (IOSA) certification is current.
recommendations: Maintain IOSA certification, ensure timely renewal of air service licences, strengthen safety reporting culture.

companyName: Passenger Rail Agency of South Africa
framework: Public Finance Management Act (PFMA) Act 1 of 1999
findings: Adverse audit opinion from Auditor-General for fifth consecutive year. Irregular expenditure exceeds R20 billion cumulatively. Material misstatements in financial statements. Consequence management is inadequate.
recommendations: Urgent intervention required: appoint qualified CFO, implement financial recovery plan, strengthen internal audit, implement Auditor-General recommendations.

companyName: Passenger Rail Agency of South Africa
framework: Legal Succession to the South African Transport Services Act 9 of 1989
findings: PRASA fails to meet minimum service level obligations. Only 40% of scheduled services operate. Metrorail services in Cape Town, Johannesburg and Durban are severely curtailed. Commuter safety incidents are above acceptable levels.
recommendations: Develop service recovery plan, prioritise high-density corridors, accelerate rolling stock renewal, improve security at stations and on trains.

framework: King IV Report on Corporate Governance
requirement: Board Leadership, Ethics and Corporate Citizenship
findings: Board has been reconstituted. Financial crisis has dominated board agenda, limiting focus on strategic governance. Ethics management programme requires strengthening. Board committee structure is in place but effectiveness is limited.
recommendations: Stabilise board composition, implement ethics hotline, strengthen board committee effectiveness, develop governance improvement plan.

framework: Public Finance Management Act (PFMA) Act 1 of 1999
findings: Disclaimer of opinion from Auditor-General. Inability to pay salaries constitutes material breach of employment obligations. Irregular expenditure of R2.1 billion. Going concern uncertainty disclosed in financial statements.
recommendations: Implement financial rescue plan, regularise salary payments, strengthen financial controls, develop credible path to financial sustainability.

companyName: South African Broadcasting Corporation SOC Limited
findings: SABC broadcasts in all 11 official languages as required. However, financial constraints have reduced local content production below licence conditions. ICASA has issued compliance notices for failure to meet local content quotas.
recommendations: Develop local content funding strategy, engage ICASA on compliance timeline, explore co-production partnerships, implement digital migration plan.

companyName: South African Broadcasting Corporation SOC Limited
framework: Public Finance Management Act (PFMA) Act 1 of 1999
findings: Qualified audit opinion received. Irregular expenditure of R1.8 billion. TV licence revenue shortfall creates structural deficit. Consequence management for prior year irregular expenditure is inadequate.
recommendations: Implement financial turnaround plan, strengthen revenue collection, implement consequence management, develop sustainable funding model.

companyName: Airports Company South Africa SOC Limited
framework: King IV Report on Corporate Governance
requirement: Board Leadership, Ethics and Corporate Citizenship
findings: ACSA demonstrates strong governance practices. Board composition meets King IV requirements. Board effectiveness evaluation conducted annually. Ethics management programme is robust. Integrated reporting is of high quality.
recommendations: Maintain current governance standards, enhance stakeholder engagement disclosures, strengthen climate risk governance.

companyName: Airports Company South Africa SOC Limited
findings: All nine managed airports comply with ICAO Annex 14 standards. SACAA audits confirm compliance with safety regulations. Security standards meet ICAO Annex 17 requirements. Emergency response plans are current and tested.
recommendations: Continue investment in safety infrastructure, maintain ICAO compliance, enhance cybersecurity for airport management systems.
